sections.question: A member of the City Planning Commission is the son, Vice-President and employee of the A. J. Diani Construction Company. His job is primarily that of public relations representative for the company. The Company contracts annually with the City of Santa Maria for the construction work of the approximate value of $500,000, including improvements in subdivisions approved by the Planning Commission and street work let by the City Council. Is this a violation of Section 87100? Also the Planning Commission awards no construction contracts and is an advisory body to the City Council in connection with land development matters. If the situation amounts to a Conflict of Interest, what procedure is necessary in order to eliminate the violation?
sections.conclusion: Based upon the information supplied in your letter of July 26, the commissioner may not make or participate in making any decisions of the planning commission on the awarding of contracts or the approval of the work product of the construction company. It is the obligation of the Commissioner to disqualify himself pursuant to Government Code 87100 and the disqualification provisions of the Conflict of Interest Code of the city planning commission whenever the decisions will have a material financial effect upon the company.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a City Planning Commissioner who is the son, vice-president, and employee of a construction company that contracts with the city violates conflict of interest laws, and if so, what procedure is necessary to eliminate the violation.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Planning Commissioner must disqualify himself from making or participating in any decisions regarding the construction company's contracts or work approval pursuant to Government Code 87100, as such decisions would have a material financial effect on the company in which he has a financial interest.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request (July 26 letter from Acting City Attorney) and FPPC response (August 1977 letter from Chief of Conflicts of Interest Division). Extracted the FPPC response section as the primary advice letter. OCR quality issues present but content is legible. Response explicitly states it does not constitute an official Commission opinion per Government Code 87100.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: A member of the City Planning Commission is the son, Vice-President and employee of the A. J. Diani Construction Company. His job is primarily that of public relations representative for the company. The Company contracts annually with the City of Santa Maria for the construction work of the approximate value of $500,000, including improvements in subdivisions approved by the Planning Commission and street work let by the City Council. Is this a violation of Section 87100? Also the Planning Commission awards no construction contracts and is an advisory body to the City Council in connection with land development matters. If the situation amounts to a Conflict of Interest, what procedure is necessary in order to eliminate the violation?

CONCLUSION: Based upon the information supplied in your letter of July 26, the commissioner may not make or participate in making any decisions of the planning commission on the awarding of contracts or the approval of the work product of the construction company. It is the obligation of the Commissioner to disqualify himself pursuant to Government Code 87100 and the disqualification provisions of the Conflict of Interest Code of the city planning commission whenever the decisions will have a material financial effect upon the company.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that a City Planning Commissioner who is employed by and has a financial interest in a construction company that contracts with the city must disqualify himself from planning commission decisions involving that company's work. The response clarifies that disqualification is required whenever such decisions would have a material financial effect on the company.
